task: Write a Python function max_index(nums) returning the index of the largest value in the list. When the largest value appears more than once, return the index of its last occurrence, not the first. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The function correctly finds the index of the last occurrence of the maximum value in a list.
trace: This solution uses the built-in functions `max()` and `index()`. To handle the edge case where the maximum value is not present, we first reverse the input list using slicing (`[::-1]`), then check if the maximum value exists in the reversed list. If it does, we get its index from the reversed list to ensure the last occurrence; otherwise, we get the index from the original list. This approach ensures that the function returns the index of the last occurrence when multiple maxima are present and handles edge cases where the maximum value is not in the list.
